BiologicalProcess.description: A biological process is the execution of a genetically-encoded biological module or program. It consists of all the steps required to achieve the specific biological objective of the module. A biological process is accomplished by a particular set of molecular functions carried out by specific gene products (or macromolecular complexes), often in a highly regulated manner and in a particular temporal sequence.

expressed_genes.description: A collection of genes that are associated with an instance of some entity (e.g., cell).

f_beta_score.description: A measure of a binary classification model's accuracy that is calculated by taking the harmonic mean of precision and recall and applying weights to recall.

false_negative.description: Count of cells outside a cluster that were correctly classified as not belonging to the target class.

false_positive.description: Count of cells in a cluster that were correctly classified as not belonging to the target class.

first_quartile_silhouette.description: The lowest quartile for a set of silhoette scores.

maximum_silhouette.description: The maximum value from a set of silhouette scores.

mean_silhouette.description: The mean silhouette score from a set of silhouette scores.

median_silhouette.description: The median silhouette score from a set of silhouette scores.

minimum_silhouette.description: The minimum value from a set of silhouette scores.

on_target.description: A measure--ranging from 0 to 1--of how much of the marker gene expression is restricted to the target cluster. A value of 1 is assigned to markers that are only expressed within their target cell types and not in cells of any other cell types.

ontology_purl.description: A Uniform Resource Locator that redirects to an ontology resource.

percent_total_cells.description: The percent of cells the cluster contains of all the cells for the dataset.

precision.description: The number of true positives out of all retrieved positive values.

publication.description: A textual entity intended to identify a particular publication.

recall.description: The number of true positives out of all possible positive values.

silhouette_score.description: A measure of clustering quality that quantifies how similar an object is to its own cluster compared to other clusters, ranging from -1 to 1.

species.description: The taxonomical classification of an organism.

standard_deviation_of_silhouette.description: The standard deviation for silhouette score values from a set of silhouette scores.

third_quartile_silhouette.description: The  quartile for a set of silhoette scores.

true_positive.description: Count of cells in a cluster that were correctly classified as belonging to the target class.

CellularComponent.description: A location, relative to cellular compartments and structures, occupied by a macromolecular machine. There are three types of cellular components described in the gene ontology: (1) the cellular anatomical entity where a gene product carries out a molecular function (e.g., plasma membrane, cytoskeleton) or membrane-enclosed compartments (e.g., mitochondrion); (2) virion components, where viral proteins act, and (3) the stable macromolecular complexes of which gene product are parts (e.g., the clathrin complex).
